line_technical_records.color_type: demi (acidic, ammonia-free, no-lift demi-permanent liquid gloss; 'the haircolor that thinks it's a conditioner')
line_technical_records.default_mixing_ratio: 1:1 (equal parts color : developer), as printed on the official US 2022 chart and the salon-services UK chart
developer_options: Shades EQ Gloss Processing Solution (official US 2022 chart: 'Use only with Shades EQ Gloss Processing Solution or Processing Solution for Precision Application')
Shades EQ Processing Solution for Precision Application (official US 2022 chart)
Gloss to Gel Processing Solution / Gloss to Gel Developer - transforms Shades EQ into a gel texture for precision application (salon-services UK chart)
developer_strengths_if_stated: Gloss to Gel Developer described as '2% hydrogen peroxide specifically formulated to maintain an acidic pH' on the salon-services.com product listing (search extract, not from the fetched official chart)
processing_time_ranges: 20 minutes at room temperature - standard processing for gloss, tone, refresh, correct, add dimension (official US 2022 chart '20 minute processing'; salon-services chart: 'Develop for 20 minutes')
Gray blending, resistant hair, more vibrant results: process 15 minutes with plastic cap under a pre-heated warm dryer, then remove cap and cool 5 minutes at room temperature (official US 2022 chart)
Resistant gray: 20 minutes with plastic cap under pre-heated warm dryer, then additional 20 minutes at room temperature (official Redken AU chart, 2013)
20 minutes, up to 40 minutes for gray hair (official Redken AU chart, 2013, mixing table)
Lighter color deposit / porous hair: process for the full recommended time at room temperature using 000 Crystal Clear in the formula or 09/010 level shades (official US 2022 chart)
gray_coverage_rules: Gray blending option per the official US 2022 processing table (15 minutes capped under pre-heated warm dryer + 5 minutes cool).
Can 'blend or color gray, without lifting the hair's natural pigment' (official Redken AU chart, 2013).
lift_rules: No lift: the leading '0' in every shade code indicates no lift (salon-services UK chart label diagram).
No-lift, no-ammonia formula; won't lighten hair's natural pigment (official Redken AU chart, 2013).
Levels of lift: none (official Redken AU chart mixing table, 2013).

tone_family_legend.meaning: Titanium (printed tone: Silver/Blue)
Blue (printed tone: Blue)
Violet (printed tone: Violet)
Green Natural (printed tone: Green/Yellow)
Natural (printed tone: Blue/Violet)
Neutral Brown Blonde (printed tone: Red/Violet)
Gold Beige (printed tone: Gold/Mauve)
Gold (printed tone: Yellow)
Gold Gold (printed tone: Yellow Yellow)
Warm Gold (printed tone: Yellow/Orange)
Copper (printed tone: Orange)
Copper Copper (printed tone: Orange/Orange)
Copper Brown (printed tone: Red/Orange)
Red Brown (printed tone: Red)
Red (printed tone: Red)
Red Violet (printed tone: Red/Violet)
Mahogany Violet (printed tone: Violet/Red)

special_usage_notes: Dilution table (salon-services UK chart): Full Strength 60ml shade + 0ml Crystal Clear + 60ml Processing Solution; Minimal Dilution 45ml + 15ml + 60ml; Moderate Dilution 30ml + 30ml + 60ml; Maximum Dilution 15ml + 45ml + 60ml. Shades EQ can be diluted with Crystal Clear (000) or a 09 shade to lessen intensity or lighten tone - for toning, balayage and blondes.

The level of deposit and intensity of any formula can be adjusted by mixing lighter shades with darker shades or by diluting with 000 Crystal Clear, 09, or 010 light level shades (official US 2022 chart).

Three background types (official US 2022 chart): Black to Gray (cool foundation to diminish warmth), Brown to Tan (foundation for balanced natural results), No Background (pure reflect shades for vibrant true-to-tone results).

Deposit groupings (official US 2022 chart): 07/08/09/010 shades have the least pigment (lightest deposit); 04/05/06 shades are medium deposit; 01/02/03 shades have the greatest pigment and deepest deposit.
Pastels: end results depend on starting level - Silver Green & Pink: Level 9+; Peach: Level 8+; Aqua Blue: Level 7+ (official US 2022 chart).
Color Kickers are vibrant, high-purity shades with no added brown or background, used as additives to enhance other formulas (official US 2022 chart).

Traditional level system table printed on official US 2022 chart (level / tone / undertone / corrective): 10 Ultra Pale Blonde - Pale Yellow - Violet; 9 Very Light Blonde - Yellow - Violet; 8 Light Blonde - Yellow/Orange - Blue/Violet; 7 Medium Blonde - Orange - Blue; 6 Dark Blonde - Red/Orange - Blue Green; 5 Light Brown - Red - Green; 4 Medium Brown - Red/Violet - Yellow Green; 3 Dark Brown - Violet; 2 Darkest Brown - Blue/Violet; 1 Black - Blue.

Shades EQ Bonder Inside: 'patent pending acidic liquid technology, infused with Bonder Inside for added strength'; some shades available in both Shades EQ Gloss and Shades EQ Bonder Inside, some only in Bonder Inside (official US 2022 chart key).
Allergy alert test must be done 48 hours before each application (salon-services UK chart).

Usage (official AU chart, 2013): glaze (enhance/enrich/change/match/tone/intensify; tone pre-lightened hair), refresh (Zones 2 and 3 while permanent color processes at regrowth), correct (fill before tintback, correct brassy tones, lowlight).
line_technical_records.discontinued_status: Active - line listed on redkenpro.com and on the official shade-chart hub as of June 2026.
line_technical_records.source_document: Official Redken 2022 Shades EQ Bonder Inside mini shade chart NA (redkenpro.com PDF, copyright 2021); supplemented by salon-services.com UK A5 booklet Shades EQ chart (https://www.salon-services.com/on/demandware.static/-/Sites-salon-services-Library/default/dwae838b2f/410508%20Redken%20A5%20Booklet%20Shades%20EQ%20Chart.pdf), official Redken AU Shades EQ mini chart 2013 (https://www.redken.com.au/-/media/project/loreal/brand-sites/redken/apac/au/shade-chart-pdfs/seq_gloss_minichart_u1149800.pdf), and redkenpro.com shade-chart hub page
assumptions: Tone-family legend codes/meanings were read from the official Redken Australia mini chart (2013), the only fetched source that prints code-to-family-name pairings explicitly; family codes are consistent with the US chart shade codes.
Dilution table and 48-hour allergy test wording were read from the UK distributor (salon-services.com) chart, not a US official document.
data_gaps: US official tone-family legend pairings could not be extracted for codes NA, NW, P, M, VB, AG, GI, NCh, VV, CR, ABn, VRo, AA, A, RR (column alignment lost in PDF text extraction).
No parseable official chart newer than the 2022 chart (copyright 2021) was obtained; shades launched 2023-2026 may be missing.
Developer strengths for Shades EQ Gloss Processing Solution not stated in fetched official sources.
1001 Blondes subset chart PDF could not be fetched (bot-challenge page).

line_technical_records.color_type: permanent (high saturation permanent liquid lacquer; low ammonia)
line_technical_records.default_mixing_ratio: 1:1 (color : developer), per the Mixing & Processing table on both official 2020 charts
processing_time_ranges: 10 volume: 20 minutes (minimal lift, up to 1 level)
20 volume: 30-35 minutes per mini chart / 30 minutes per full-size chart (standard lift / gray coverage, up to 2 levels)
30 volume: 40 minutes (additional lift, up to 3 levels)
Off-scalp / heat option: do not use heat (Mixing & Processing table)
gray_coverage_rules: Provides exceptional gray coverage, from a few strands to high percentages of gray (official 2020 full-size chart).
7 families can be used alone for superior gray coverage up to 100% gray: ABn (Ash Brown), NN (Natural Natural), N (Natural), NW (Natural Warm), WG (Warm Gold), CB (Copper Brown), RB (Ruby Brown).
To maintain the most control when covering gray, select a formula no more than two levels lighter than the natural hair.

Coverage proportion table - Cooling families (GN, AB, NA) mixed with ABn, N or NN; Warming families (GB, NG, RO) mixed with NW, WG, CB or RB: up to 25% gray = 0 + 2 oz (cooling) or 1/2 oz + 1 1/2 oz (warming); 25-50% = 1/2 oz + 1 1/2 oz or 3/4 oz + 1 1/4 oz; 50-75% = 1 oz + 1 oz (both); 75-100% = 1 1/2 oz + 1/2 oz (both). (Official 2020 mini chart, oz with ml equivalents.)
Determine the client's natural level using the Natural Warm (NW) family swatches in the swatch book (formulation guidelines).
Use 20 volume Pro-Oxide Cream Developer for standard lift / gray coverage.
lift_rules: 10 volume: up to 1 level of lift (minimal lift)
20 volume: up to 2 levels of lift (standard lift / gray coverage)
30 volume: up to 3 levels of lift (additional lift)

tone_family_legend.meaning: Green Natural - Controls unwanted red tones when lifting
Ash Blue - Offers maximum refinement of warmth
Natural Ash - Controls warmth when lifting while ensuring natural results
Ash Brown - Ultra-cool ash tones for the coolest brunette coverage results
Natural - Helps control warmth on pigmented hair and provides never too warm gray coverage
Natural Natural - Extreme coverage neutral tones specifically formulated for resistant grays and resistant hair textures
Gold Beige - Delivers sophisticated results that are never too warm
Natural Warm - Provides exceptional gray coverage by adding depth and warmth to non-pigmented hair
Natural Gold - Natural, soft golden shades
Warm Gold - Creates warm results with golden accents and provides excellent gray coverage
Copper Brown - Burnished copper with complete gray coverage
Ruby Brown - Delivers soft ruby-like results and excellent gray coverage
Red Orange - Accent colors with no brown in the base
Red Red - Intense reds with increased tonal depth and no brown create exciting results on naturally pigmented hair
Red Violet - Jewel tones with no added brown deliver vibrant results
Violet Rose - Deposits cool, violet-based rose tones
Color Gels Lacquers Clear - Non-pigmented; use alone when no toning is desired or use for diluting other Color Gels Lacquers shades

special_usage_notes: Over 50 shades in 13 tonal families, plus Clear; formulated with a traditional 10-level system (official 2020 full-size chart).
Select Dye System contains 100% oxidative, UV-stable, long-lasting dyes for holding power and resistance to fading.
All Carmin'R and R5 dye shades should be used alone or intermixed only with other R5 or Carmin'R shades; mixing them outside the range can cause unpredictable results (official 2020 charts).

Ash Brown (ABn) shades: blue/pearl (.19) reflect, black-to-gray background, cooler than the NN family; can be used alone or intermixed with any Color Gels Lacquers shades excluding red dye technology shades.
Backgrounds: brown-to-tan background helps ensure gray coverage; black-to-gray background helps diminish warmth when lifting; no-background shades are pure reflect (official 2020 charts).
Zone application: alkaline color (Color Gels Lacquers) only on virgin hair / Zone 1; always use acidic Shades EQ on Zones 2 and 3; 'Always refresh with Shades EQ' (official 2020 full-size chart).

Virgin application: apply formula with higher-volume developer to Zone 2 (mid-shaft) first, then same formula with lower-volume developer to Zone 1 (scalp); start timing after Zone 1 application (official 2020 full-size chart example uses 30 vol on Zone 2 and 20 vol on Zone 1).

NA and NW family tones vary by level per the color wheel (e.g. 5NW has a red tone, 5NA has a green tone); printed NA tone ladder 10NA-4NA: Violet, Violet, Blue/Violet, Blue, Blue/Green, Green, Yellow/Green; NW ladder 10NW-1NW: Pale Yellow, Yellow, Yellow/Orange, Orange, Red/Orange, Red, Red/Violet, Violet, Blue/Violet, Blue.
37 Color Gels shades have a 1:1 Shades EQ Gloss refresh match with matching shade names (official redkenpro.com product/education pages).
Allergy alert test 48 hours before each application; not intended for persons under 16; strand test recommended on porous/chemically treated hair (official 2020 full-size chart).
line_technical_records.discontinued_status: Active - line listed on redkenpro.com product page as of June 2026.
line_technical_records.source_document: Official Redken Color Gels Lacquers mini shade chart 2020 (https://www.redkenpro.com/education/-/media/project/loreal/brand%20sites/redken/americas/us_us/education/shade%20charts/color%20gels%20mini%20shade%20chart%202020.pdf) and full-size shade chart 2020 (https://www.redkenpro.com/education/-/media/project/loreal/brand%20sites/redken/americas/us_us/education/shade%20charts/color%20gels%20full%20size%20shade%20chart%202020.pdf), both redkenpro.com /education/ media PDFs ('All rights reserved. 2020'); plus official product page (https://www.redkenpro.com/products/haircolor/color-gels-lacquers) and the Color Gels Lacquers professional service page on redkenpro.com
data_gaps: Charts are dated 2020; shades or rules introduced after 2020 (if any) are not captured.
Which specific shades carry Rubilane, R5 or Carmin'R dye technology could not be mapped from extracted PDF text (visual icons only).
The exact 37-shade list of 1:1 Shades EQ Gloss refresh matches was not enumerated in fetched sources.

issue_log: The user-provided redkenpro.com media URLs (1001 Blondes chart; 2020 Color Gels Lacquers chart at the /2020-shade-charts/ path) returned bot-challenge JavaScript pages, not PDFs; equivalent official PDFs were located via search at alternate redkenpro.com media paths and fetched successfully with curl.
redkenpro.com/education/shade-charts hub page was fetched but its downloadable chart links point to a login-gated Asset Library; no direct PDF links were extractable from the HTML.
Color Gels Lacquers 20-volume processing time conflicts between official 2020 sources (30-35 min on mini chart vs 30 min on full-size chart); both values recorded.

Shades EQ line rules combine the official US 2022 chart (primary), the official Redken AU 2013 chart (tone legend, AU-specific timings flagged), and the salon-services.com UK distributor chart (dilution table); region of each statement is noted inline.
Today is June 2026 but the newest parseable official charts found were 2020 (Color Gels Lacquers) and 2021/2022 (Shades EQ); line rules may have been updated since.
